mappings.rationaleAI: Directly addresses perception and the senses including visual processing, auditory perception, multisensory integration, proprioception, and vestibular sense. Covers sensory illusions, synesthesia, philosophical theories of perception, and the relationship between sensation and interpretation.

Pain nociception, nerve pathways, gate control theory, and the subjective experience of pain directly address the cutaneous sense of pain, its cause and function, qualities, theories, and modes of treatment.

The eye and vision episode covers the anatomy of the eye from Galen to modern understanding, the mechanism of visual perception, the role of the lens and retina, and visual processing in the brain, directly addressing the work of the retina, transduction, and perceptual processes of vision.

The neural basis of perception and awareness, the role of different brain regions in cognitive functions, and the development of neuroscience from phrenology to brain imaging address physiological mechanisms of attention and cortical responses to stimuli.

Consciousness, neural correlates of awareness, and altered states of consciousness address the problem of defining attention and awareness of internal and external events. Discussion of qualia and the explanatory gap relates to hallucinatory experience and neurological factors.

The nervous system's sensory pathways, the autonomic nervous system, and its role in sensation directly address physiological mechanisms of attention, senses in general, kinesthesis, and vestibular functions.

Advances in neural networks, brain imaging technologies, and the understanding of consciousness address physiological mechanisms of attention, responses of the cerebral cortex to visual stimuli, and contemporary theories of perception.

Echolocation covers the physics of sound reflection, neurological processing of echoes, and auditory perception, directly addressing audition mechanisms and nonvisual (auditory) cues for perceiving movement.

Berkeley's immaterialist philosophy that objects exist only as perceptions (esse est percipi) and his work on vision and optics directly address theories of perception, perceptual organization, and illusory experience.

mappings.episodeTitle: Goethe and the Science of the Enlightenment
mappings.rationaleAI: Goethe's Theory of Colours arguing for the primacy of subjective perception in understanding light and color directly addresses perceptual processes of vision, context effects, and the influence of surrounding stimuli on observation.

The mind-body problem addresses the relationship between mental states and physical states, directly relevant to the problem of defining attention and consciousness, and central problems of perception theory including the explanatory gap between brain processes and phenomenal experience.

Kant's proposal that the mind actively structures experience through a priori categories of space and time directly addresses theories of perceptual organization, perceptual constancy, and the nature of space perception.

Hypnosis covers trance states, altered consciousness, the debate over distinct neurological states, and suggestibility, directly relevant to hallucinations and factors affecting time perception such as hypnosis.
